title: Firecrawl Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the Firecrawl web scraping API monorepo — covering the API server, scraping engines, services, SDKs, and infrastructure.

steps.title: Welcome to Firecrawl
steps.description: Firecrawl is an open-source web scraping API that turns websites into clean, structured data. This monorepo contains the API server, multiple SDK clients, supporting microservices, and infrastructure configuration. Let's explore how it all fits together.

steps.title: API Server — Entry Point & Routing
steps.description: The API lives in apps api and is an Express server. Requests enter through src index.ts, get routed through versioned route files, and land in corresponding controllers. The API supports three versions simultaneously, with v2 being the current primary.

steps.title: Scraping Engines
steps.description: The heart of Firecrawl: multiple scraping strategies live under scraper/scrapeURL/engines. Fire Engine is the proprietary engine, Playwright handles JavaScript-heavy pages, Fetch does simple HTTP requests, and specialized engines handle PDFs and documents. The scraper orchestrator in scrapeURL/index.ts selects the best engine per request.

steps.title: Services & Workers
steps.description: Services handle the business logic and async job processing. Scrape/crawl requests are queued via BullMQ (Redis-backed) and processed by dedicated workers. The worker directory contains the scrape worker, crawl logic, and concurrency controls. Supporting services handle webhooks, billing, notifications, and monitoring.

steps.title: Extraction Pipeline & Libraries
steps.description: The lib directory contains shared utilities and the AI-powered extraction pipeline. The extract/ subdirectory builds LLM prompts, processes responses, and re-ranks results. Other key utilities handle HTML-to-Markdown conversion, URL validation, crawl state management in Redis, deep research, and LLM.txt generation.

steps.title: Client SDKs
steps.description: Firecrawl ships SDKs in JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, and Rust (plus a Java SDK). Each SDK mirrors the API surface with versioned clients (v1, v2), typed method wrappers for scrape/crawl/extract/search/batch, and utilities for pagination and job watching. The JS and Python SDKs also include async support.

steps.title: Supporting Microservices
steps.description: Two standalone microservices support the main API. The Playwright service (TypeScript/Express) provides browser-based rendering for JavaScript-heavy pages with ad-blocking and user-agent rotation. The Go HTML-to-Markdown service efficiently converts scraped HTML into clean Markdown at scale.

steps.title: Testing Strategy
steps.description: Firecrawl uses 'snips' — end-to-end tests organized by API version under __tests__/snips/. Each endpoint has its own test file covering happy and failure paths. Tests run via 'pnpm harness jest' which spins up the API server and workers. The separate test-suite app holds integration and load tests.

steps.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
steps.description: The docker-compose.yaml orchestrates the full stack: API, Playwright service, Redis, RabbitMQ, and PostgreSQL (NUQ). Each app has its own Dockerfile. Configuration flows through environment variables defined in .env files, with Zod schema validation in src/config.ts. The SELF_HOST.md guide covers self-hosting setup.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: You've now seen the complete Firecrawl architecture: an Express API with versioned routes and controllers, a multi-engine scraper with AI-powered extraction, async job processing via BullMQ, three client SDKs, supporting microservices, and containerized deployment. Explore the codebase and start building!
